geographic_restrictions: Primarily limited to non-profit organizations in New Hampshire, with emphasis on Hillsborough County.
project_area: Arts and culture, camps, child welfare, Christianity, crisis intervention, developmental disability services, education, environment, health, historic preservation, hospice care, human services, mental health counseling, residential mental health care, secondary education, youth organizing, youth services
demographic_requirements: Projects serving adults, children and youth, families, and low-income people are of special interest.
eligibility_summary: Giving is limited to non-profit organizations in New Hampshire (emphasis on Hillsborough County). No support for public schools. No grants to individuals or loans. Applications must be submitted by March 15 and September 15 annually. Initial approach should be with an application. Required materials include organizational/project budget, IRS Determination Letter, expected results, sustainability plan, project description, and funding request amount.
